title: Glencore closes agreement with Gécamines for land access at KCC
gist: Brief Glencore press release (27 May 2026) confirming that land-lease agreements for Kamoto Copper Company in the DRC have been registered at the mining cadastre and the deal with Gécamines has closed; thin content with no financial terms disclosed.

themes.theme: DRC copper asset land access secured

insights.claim: Glencore confirmed on 27 May 2026 that the land-lease agreements for Kamoto Copper Company (KCC) with Gécamines have been registered at the mining cadastre and the agreement has now closed, following the original 18 February announcement.
insights.speaker: Glencore investor reports
insights.so_what: Removes a land-access overhang at a key DRC copper/cobalt operation; reduces operational uncertainty for Glencore's KCC output, relevant to copper supply watchers.
insights.quote: Glencore confirms that the lease agreements relating to the land package have been registered at the mining cadastre and that the agreement has now closed.
insights.locator: Further to the announcement on 18 February
